theme: Light mode default, dark mode supported via toggle.
rationale: For an enterprise logistics system like Quimbar, trust, precision, and authority are paramount. We use a Swiss & High-Contrast archetype. The design will rely on crisp 1px borders, structural grayscale, and a sharp signal blue accent to convey an 'elite command center' feel, avoiding fluffy generic SaaS aesthetics.

rules: Use 'Cabinet Grotesk' or 'Clash Display' for headings. Fallback to a geometric sans if unavailable.
Use 'IBM Plex Sans' for body text to give a technical, data-driven feel.
Headlines > 32px MUST use tight tracking (tracking-tight or tracking-tighter).
Overlines (e.g., 'QUIMBAR LOGISTICS') MUST be Uppercase, text-xs, with wide tracking (tracking-[0.2em]).
Do NOT center-align everything. Left-align dense content, pricing, and features for optical clarity.
H1 should scale fluidly up to text-5xl or text-6xl, no larger.

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Generous spacing. Use p-8, p-12, p-24 for section paddings.
spacing_and_layout.grid_strategy: Use the 'Control Room' grid for the pricing calculator and 'Tetris' Bento grid for features.
spacing_and_layout.borders: Adopt the 'Grid Borders' look. Use 1px solid borders (border-gray-200 / border-zinc-800) heavily to separate structural elements instead of relying on soft shadows.
spacing_and_layout.radius: Keep corners sharp (rounded-none) or very subtle (rounded-sm). Maximum radius 4px to maintain the rigid, technical enterprise feel.

components_strategy.hero: Use the generated architectural logistics image. Must have a gradient overlay (bg-gradient-to-r from-black/80 to-transparent) if used as a background to ensure text contrast.
components_strategy.pricing_calculator: Highly interactive. Must include: 1) A range slider or number input for License quantity. 2) A toggle switch for 'Premium Add-on'. 3) A toggle for Monthly vs Annual billing (with a visible discount badge). 4) A fixed/sticky 'Total Calculation' pane that updates in real-time.
components_strategy.plan_cards: 3 side-by-side cards (Esencial, Operativo, Empresarial) using a border-collapse grid style. Highlight the 'Operativo' plan with a 'Tracing Beam' or primary border highlight.
components_strategy.forms_and_modals: Use Shadcn UI Dialog for the 'Solicitar licencia' flow. The form should preview the calculated total before submitting via mailto.

animations_and_interactions.hover_states: All buttons must have explicit hover states (e.g., hover:bg-blue-700 hover:text-white).
animations_and_interactions.transitions: Keep animations minimalist. Use simple opacity/transform fades (duration-200 ease-in-out) for the pricing numbers when they recalculate.
animations_and_interactions.accessibility: Ensure focus states use ring-2 ring-primary ring-offset-2.

icons_and_assets.icons: Use Phosphor Icons (@phosphor-icons/react) or Lucide-react for sharp, consistent, technical iconography. Ensure line weights match.

hero_background.description: High-contrast architectural shipping terminal image for the main hero section.

feature_truck.description: Logistics truck on highway for the 'Transport Management' feature block.

feature_warehouse.description: Modern warehouse interior for the 'Inventory & Logistics' block.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Initialize a React project with Tailwind CSS and Shadcn UI (Slider, Switch, Dialog, Tabs, Card, Button, Input, Label).
2. Implement a Theme toggle (light/dark) in the Header.

3. Build the 'Quimbar' Pricing Calculator: Base installation is fixed at $3,600. Add inputs for licenses (monthly vs annual applies a discount) and a switch for the Premium add-on. Display the real-time sum in a prominent sticky or side-panel summary.
4. Create 3 Plan Cards (Esencial, Operativo, Empresarial) with their respective features.

5. The 'Solicitar licencia' button should open a Modal with a summary of their configuration. The submit button in this modal MUST trigger a 'mailto:' link containing the selected plan, license count, premium status, and total price in the email body.
6. Apply E1 design rules strictly: no generic blob shapes, no purple/teal gradients. Use strict 1px grid borders, stark contrast, and the specified blue primary color.
7. Include 'data-testid' attributes on all interactive elements (buttons, sliders, toggles, form inputs).
